4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Duplicate @types/react (v18 vs v19)**: Added `pnpm.overrides` in package.json to force `@types/react@^19.2.14`. This resolved the `'Check' cannot be used as a JSX component` typecheck error in packages/ui components using lucide-react icons.
   
   - **useSearchParams() Suspense boundary**: Next.js 15 requires `useSearchParams()` to be wrapped in `<Suspense>` for static prerendering. Split LoginPage into wrapper + LoginForm inner component.
   
   - **docker-compose.storage.yml invalid interpolation**: `${STORAGE_ENDPOINT##*:}` is bash parameter expansion that doesn't work in Docker Compose. Fixed by adding `STORAGE_PORT=44877` to .env.dev and using `${STORAGE_PORT}` in the compose file.
   
   - **docker-compose.pgadmin.yml cross-file depends_on**: `depends_on: - postgres` fails because postgres is defined in a different compose file. Removed the depends_on block (start order handled by start.sh).
   
   - **docker-compose.app.yml cross-file depends_on**: Same issue — removed depends_on for postgres and valkey from app compose.
   
   - **pgAdmin invalid email**: pgAdmin rejects `.local` TLD emails. Changed `PGADMIN_EMAIL` from `inventorize-d76105f4@admin.local` to `admin@inventorize.dev`. Had to remove the pgadmin volume and recreate.
   
   - **CURRENT/UNRESOLVED: Prisma in Edge Runtime**: middleware.ts imports `auth` from `@/server/auth` which imports `platformPrisma` from `@inventorize/db`. Prisma's `Extensions.defineExtension` cannot run in Edge Runtime. The middleware needs to be rewritten to avoid Prisma imports — either by using a lightweight JWT check or by configuring the middleware to run in Node.js runtime.

1	// Auth.js v5 configuration — credentials provider with PostgreSQL session storage
2	// Sessions stored in PostgreSQL via Prisma adapter (white-label, zero external auth service)
3	
4	import NextAuth from 'next-auth';
5	import Credentials from 'next-auth/providers/credentials';
6	import { compare } from 'bcryptjs';
7	import { platformPrisma } from '@inventorize/db';
8	import type { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';
9	
10	export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({
11	  providers: [
12	    Credentials({
13	      name: 'credentials',
14	      credentials: {
15	        email: { label: 'Email', type: 'email' },
16	        password: { label: 'Password', type: 'password' },
17	      },
18	      async authorize(credentials) {
19	        if (typeof credentials?.email !== 'string' || typeof credentials?.password !== 'string') {
20	          return null;
21	        }
22	
23	        const user = await platformPrisma.user.findFirst({
24	          where: { email: credentials.email, isActive: true },
25	          include: { tenant: true },
26	        });
27	
28	        if (user === null) {
29	          return null;
30	        }
31	
32	        const isValid = await compare(credentials.password, user.hashedPassword);
33	        if (!isValid) {
34	          return null;
35	        }
36	
37	        // Update last login
38	        await platformPrisma.user.update({
39	          where: { id: user.id },
40	          data: { lastLoginAt: new Date() },
41	        });
42	
43	        return {
44	          id: user.id,
45	          name: user.name,
46	          email: user.email,
47	          role: user.role as UserRole,
48	          tenantId: user.tenantId,
49	          tenantSlug: user.tenant?.slug ?? null,
50	        };
51	      },
52	    }),
53	  ],
54	  pages: {
55	    signIn: '/login',
56	    error: '/login',
57	  },
58	  session: {
59	    strategy: 'jwt',
60	    maxAge: 8 * 60 * 60, // 8 hours
61	  },
62	  callbacks: {
63	    jwt({ token, user }) {
64	      if (user !== undefined && user !== null) {
65	        token.userId = user.id;
66	        token.role = (user as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)['role'] as string;
67	        token.tenantId = (user as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantId'] as string | null;
68	        token.tenantSlug = (user as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantSlug'] as string | null;
69	      }
70	      return token;
71	    },
72	    session({ session, token }) {
73	      if (session.user !== undefined && session.user !== null) {
74	        session.user.id = token.userId as string;
75	        (session.user as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)['role'] = token.role;

77	        (session.user as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantSlug'] = token.tenantSlug;
78	      }
79	      return session;

```typescript
// Next.js middleware — tenant resolution from URL path, auth guard, session-URL cross-check
// Uses getToken() from next-auth/jwt which is Edge Runtime compatible (no Prisma dependency)

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { getToken } from 'next-auth/jwt';

// Paths that don't require authentication
const PUBLIC_PATHS = ['/login', '/api/health', '/api/trpc'];

export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;

  // Allow public paths
  if (PUBLIC_PATHS.some((p) => pathname.startsWith(p))) {
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  // Allow static assets and Next.js internals
  if (pathname.startsWith('/_next') || pathname.startsWith('/favicon')) {
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  // Decode JWT from cookie — Edge-compatible, no Prisma needed
  const token = await getToken({ req, secret: process.env.AUTH_SECRET });

  // Unauthenticated → redirect to login
  if (token === null) {
    const loginUrl = new URL('/login', req.url);
    loginUrl.searchParams.set('callbackUrl', pathname);
    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
  }

  const role = token.role as string | undefined;
  const tenantSlug = token.tenantSlug as string | null | undefined;

  // Super admin platform routes
  if (pathname.startsWith('/platform')) {
    if (role !== 'SUPER_ADMIN') {
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
    }
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  // Tenant-scoped routes: /[tenantSlug]/...
  const slugMatch = pathname.match(/^\/([^/]+)/);
  if (slugMatch !== null) {
    const urlSlug = slugMatch[1];

    // Cross-check: session tenantSlug must match URL slug
    // Prevents tenant-switching attack (user types /other-tenant/... in URL bar)
    if (tenantSlug !== urlSlug) {
      // Redirect to user's actual tenant
      if (tenantSlug !== null && tenantSlug !== undefined) {
        return NextResponse.redirect(new URL(`/${tenantSlug}/dashboard`, req.url));
      }
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
    }
  }

  return NextResponse.next();
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'],
};

```

Phase 6 complete. Here's the summary:

---

**Phase 6 complete. Your app is live.**

```
App:          http://localhost:3000        (standalone server)
pgAdmin:      http://localhost:44881       (admin@inventorize.dev)
MinIO Console: http://localhost:44878
MailHog UI:   http://localhost:44880
```

**6 errors fixed during Phase 6:**
1. `docker-compose.storage.yml` — bash parameter expansion doesn't work in Compose (added `STORAGE_PORT` env var)
2. `docker-compose.pgadmin.yml` / `docker-compose.app.yml` — cross-file `depends_on` invalid (removed, rely on start.sh ordering)
3. pgAdmin rejects `.local` TLD emails (changed to `admin@inventorize.dev`)
4. **middleware.ts imports Prisma via auth() wrapper — crashes in Edge Runtime** (rewrote to use `getToken()` from `next-auth/jwt`)
5. Auth.js `UntrustedHost` error on localhost (added `trustHost: true`)
6. Prisma engine binary not in standalone output (manual copy after build)

**Note:** The app runs via `node server.js` (standalone) on port 3000, not via Docker container on 44884. The Docker app compose file would build the image and map 44884→3000, but for dev iteration the standalone server is faster.

**Next steps:**
- To add features: edit `docs/PRODUCT.md` → say "Feature Update"
- To start Phase 8 iterative buildout: say "Start Phase 8"
- All commands: see `COMMANDS.md`

 `./docs/PRODUCT.md`

```text
1	# Inventorize
2	
3	## App Identity
4	Name:           Inventorize
5	Tagline:        Track your inventory, anytime, anywhere.
6	Industry:       SaaS / internal operations / inventory management
7	Primary users:  Platform super admin, tenant admins, warehouse staff, purchasing staff
8	
9	## Problem Statement
10	Small and mid-size companies still track inventory in spreadsheets — leading to stock discrepancies, no audit trail, and zero visibility into stock movements. Existing inventory software is either too expensive, too complex, or requires on-premise installation. Inventorize provides a multi-tenant SaaS where each company gets its own isolated inventory workspace with barcode scanning, serial tracking, purchase orders, stock movement history, and full audit logging — all from a browser. The platform operator manages tenant onboarding, billing readiness, and cross-tenant health monitoring from a super admin dashboard.
11	
12	## Core User Flows
13	
14	1. **Super admin onboards a new tenant**: Super admin opens tenant management → creates new tenant (name, slug, contact email) → system provisions tenant workspace with subdirectory route (app.com/tenant-slug/) → super admin creates the tenant's first admin user account with tenant assignment → tenant admin receives welcome email → logs in and begins setup. Error: duplicate slug → block, show specific error. Error: user creation fails → roll back tenant record, show retry option.
15	
16	2. **Super admin manages the platform**: Super admin views all tenants list with status (active/suspended/trial) → can suspend or reactivate a tenant → can view cross-tenant metrics (total tenants, total products across platform, active users per tenant) → can impersonate a tenant admin for support (read-only mode) → all super admin actions create AuditLog entries. Error: suspending a tenant with active users → confirm prompt before proceeding.
17	
18	3. **Tenant admin creates a product**: Tenant admin opens product creation form → enters productCode, barcodeValue, name, category, unit, supplierCost, markupPercent, lowStockThreshold, serialTrackingEnabled → system auto-calculates sellingPrice → save with currentQuantity = 0 → AuditLog created with tenantId. All data scoped to tenant automatically via RLS. Error: duplicate productCode within tenant → block save. Error: duplicate barcodeValue within tenant → block save.
19	
20	4. **Tenant admin edits a product**: Open existing product → edit allowed fields → recalculate sellingPrice if pricing changed → save → AuditLog with before/after values. Error: invalid values → block save. Note: currentQuantity never editable through this form.
21	
22	5. **Tenant admin or purchasing staff creates a purchase order**: Select supplier (tenant-scoped) → add products with ordered quantities → system snapshots supplierCost → optionally upload attachment → save as "draft" → later update status (ordered → partially_received → received, or cancel) → AuditLog on create and every status change. Error: no items → block. Error: attachment upload fails → preserve PO data, allow retry.
23	
24	6. **Any tenant staff records stock in**: Select product manually or scan product barcode via browser camera → enter quantity → optionally link PO → if serialTrackingEnabled, scan/enter serial numbers (count must equal quantity) → optionally upload delivery receipt → system validates → increase Product.currentQuantity atomically → create SerialNumber records as "in_stock" if serial-tracked → create StockMovementLog → AuditLog → if linked to PO, update receivedQty and PO status. Error: duplicate serial for product within tenant → block. Error: any failure → roll back entire transaction.
25	
26	7. **Tenant admin or warehouse staff records stock out**: Select product manually or scan barcode → enter quantity, requestedByName, usedFor, optional notes → if serialTrackingEnabled, scan/select exact serials (must be "in_stock", count must equal quantity) → validate sufficient stock → decrease Product.currentQuantity atomically → update serial status to "issued" → StockMovementLog → AuditLog → optionally generate printable slip. Error: insufficient stock → block, show available. Error: serial not in_stock → block. Error: any failure → roll back.
27	
28	8. **Tenant admin performs stock adjustment**: Enter reason and notes → select product → enter quantityDelta → if serial-tracked, identify serials → validate resulting stock not negative → apply atomically → StockMovementLog → AuditLog. approvedByUserId is optional audit trail only. Error: negative result → block. Error: any failure → roll back.
29	
30	9. **Any tenant user views product history**: Open product detail → view info, current quantity, serials if enabled → view StockMovementLog → filter by date, type, user, reference. Admin sees AuditLog entries. Pricing visibility enforced by role. All data tenant-scoped — users never see other tenants' data.
31	
32	10. **System runs low stock monitoring per tenant**: Daily scheduled job checks products per tenant where currentQuantity <= lowStockThreshold → per-tenant dashboard alert → low stock report → daily email to tenant admin and purchasing_staff → LowStockNotificationLog. Error: email fails → retry 3× with exponential backoff.
33	
34	11. **Any tenant user runs reports and exports**: Open dashboard or reports → apply filters → view data per role permissions → export as CSV → AuditLog for every export. All report data is tenant-scoped.
35	
36	## Modules + Features
37	
38	### Platform Administration (super_admin only)
39	- Tenant list with search, filter by status (active/suspended/trial)
40	- Tenant create form (name, slug, contact email)
41	- Tenant suspend / reactivate
42	- Cross-tenant metrics dashboard (total tenants, total products, active users per tenant)
43	- Tenant admin impersonation (read-only support mode)
44	- Platform-level audit log viewer
45	
46	### Dashboard (per tenant)
47	- KPI summary cards: total active products, total stock quantity, low stock count, stock in/out counts for period, inventory value (admin/purchasing only)
48	- Stock In vs Stock Out bar chart by period
49	- Low stock alert banner
50	
51	### Products
52	- Product list with search, filter, sort, pagination (tenant-scoped)
53	- Product create/edit form (tenant admin only)
54	- Product detail page with history tab
55	- Barcode scanning for quick product lookup
56	- Serial number list per product (if serial-tracked)
57	
58	### Suppliers
59	- Supplier list with search (tenant admin and purchasing_staff)
60	- Supplier create/edit form (tenant-scoped)
61	
62	### Purchase Orders
63	- PO list with status filter (tenant admin and purchasing_staff)
64	- PO create form with line items and attachment upload
65	- PO detail page with status management
66	- PO receiving (links to stock in)
67	
68	### Stock In
69	- Stock in form with barcode scanning
70	- PO linkage (optional)
71	- Serial number entry for serial-tracked products
72	- Delivery receipt upload
73	- Stock in history list
74	
75	### Stock Out
76	- Stock out form with barcode scanning
77	- Serial selection for serial-tracked products
78	- requestedByName and usedFor fields
79	- Printable stock out slip
80	- Stock out history list
81	
82	### Stock Adjustments
83	- Adjustment form (tenant admin only)
84	- Reason and notes required
85	- Positive or negative quantity delta
86	- Serial identification when applicable
87	- Adjustment history list
88	
89	### Reports
90	- Low Stock Report
91	- Stock Movement Report (filterable by date, product, type, user)
92	- Current Inventory Report
93	- Product History Report
94	- Audit Trail Report (tenant admin only, searchable)
95	- CSV export for all reports
96	
97	### Audit Logs
98	- Searchable audit log viewer (tenant admin only — tenant-scoped)
99	- Filter by action type, entity, user, date
100	
101	### Users
102	- User list (tenant admin only — shows only tenant's users)
103	- User create/edit/disable (no hard delete) — managed via Auth.js v5 with sessions in PostgreSQL
104	- Role assignment within tenant
105	
106	## Roles + Permissions
107	
108	| Role | Scope | Can do | Cannot do |
109	|------|-------|--------|-----------|
110	| super_admin | Platform-wide | Manage all tenants (create, suspend, reactivate). View cross-tenant metrics. Impersonate tenant admin (read-only). View platform audit logs. Create first admin user for new tenants. | Cannot modify tenant business data (products, stock, POs). Cannot create stock transactions. Cannot hard-delete tenants. |
111	| admin | Tenant-scoped | Manage users within own tenant (create, update, disable). Manage products (create, edit). View and update all pricing. Manage suppliers. Manage purchase orders. Create stock in/out. Create stock adjustments. View audit logs (own tenant only). View reports and export CSV. | Cannot see other tenants' data. Cannot manage platform. Cannot hard-delete users or products. Cannot directly edit currentQuantity. |
112	| warehouse_staff | Tenant-scoped | View products (selling price only). Create stock in. Create stock out. View stock movement logs. View reports and export CSV. | Cannot view supplierCost or markupPercent. Cannot manage users, suppliers, POs, or adjustments. Cannot view audit logs. Cannot see other tenants' data. |
113	| purchasing_staff | Tenant-scoped | View products with all pricing. Manage suppliers. Manage purchase orders. Create stock in. View stock out (read only). View stock movement logs. View reports and export CSV. | Cannot create stock out. Cannot manage users or adjustments. Cannot view audit logs. Cannot see other tenants' data. |
114	
115	## Data Entities
116	
117	### Platform-level (no tenantId)
118	- Tenant: id, name, slug (unique), contactEmail, status (enum: active/suspended/trial), createdAt, updatedAt
119	- User: id, tenantId → Tenant (null for super_admin), name, email, hashedPassword, role (enum: super_admin/admin/warehouse_staff/purchasing_staff), isActive, lastLoginAt, createdAt, updatedAt
120	
121	### Tenant-scoped (all have tenantId, enforced by RLS)
122	- Product: id, tenantId → Tenant, productCode (unique per tenant), barcodeValue (unique per tenant), name, category, unit, supplierCost, markupPercent, sellingPrice (computed), currentQuantity (system-managed), lowStockThreshold, serialTrackingEnabled, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
123	- Supplier: id, tenantId → Tenant, name, contactPerson, phone, email, address, notes, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
124	- PurchaseOrder: id, tenantId → Tenant, poNumber (unique per tenant), supplierId → Supplier, orderDate, expectedDate, status (enum: draft/ordered/partially_received/received/cancelled), notes, attachmentUrl, createdByUserId → User, createdAt, updatedAt
125	- PurchaseOrderItem: id, purchaseOrderId → PurchaseOrder, productId → Product, orderedQty, receivedQty, supplierCostSnapshot
126	- StockIn: id, tenantId → Tenant, referenceNumber (unique per tenant), purchaseOrderId → PurchaseOrder (optional), receivedDate, receivedByUserId → User, notes, attachmentUrl, createdAt
127	- StockInItem: id, stockInId → StockIn, productId → Product, quantity, supplierCostSnapshot
128	- StockOut: id, tenantId → Tenant, referenceNumber (unique per tenant), releasedDate, releasedByUserId → User, requestedByName, usedFor, notes, printableSlipNumber, createdAt
129	- StockOutItem: id, stockOutId → StockOut, productId → Product, quantity, sellingPriceSnapshot
130	- SerialNumber: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, serialValue, barcodeValue, status (enum: in_stock/issued/adjusted), stockInItemId → StockInItem (optional), stockOutItemId → StockOutItem (optional), createdAt, updatedAt. Unique constraint: [tenantId, productId, serialValue].
131	- StockAdjustment: id, tenantId → Tenant, adjustmentDate, reason, notes, createdByUserId → User, approvedByUserId → User (optional — audit trail only, not blocking), createdAt
132	- StockAdjustmentItem: id, stockAdjustmentId → StockAdjustment, productId → Product, quantityDelta, serialNumberId → SerialNumber (optional)
133	- StockMovementLog: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, movementType (enum: stock_in/stock_out/adjustment), referenceType, referenceId, quantityBefore, quantityDelta, quantityAfter, serialNumberId (optional), performedByUserId → User, requestedByName, usedFor, performedAt, notes — IMMUTABLE
134	- AuditLog: id, tenantId → Tenant (null for platform-level actions), actorUserId → User, actionType, entityType, entityId, fieldChangesJson, beforeStateJson, afterStateJson, createdAt — IMMUTABLE
135	- LowStockNotificationLog: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, notifiedToUserId → User, sentAt, status
136	
137	### Key Business Rules (CRITICAL — agents must enforce)
138	- Product.currentQuantity is system-managed only — never directly editable
139	- Product.sellingPrice is always computed: supplierCost + (supplierCost × markupPercent / 100)
140	- Stock can never go negative — any violating transaction must be blocked
141	- All stock quantity changes must be atomic — full transaction rollback on any failure
142	- Every quantity change must create a StockMovementLog with before/after values
143	- Every important action must create an AuditLog entry — immutable
144	- All tenant-scoped data must include tenantId — enforced by RLS at database level
145	- Users can never see, query, or modify another tenant's data
146	- Unique constraints (productCode, barcodeValue, poNumber, referenceNumber, serialValue) are scoped per tenant, not globally
147	- Serial tracking applies only when product has serialTrackingEnabled = true
148	- Barcode/QR scanning is input helper only — all scanned values must pass server-side validation
149	
150	## Integrations
151	- Email (SMTP): daily low stock notification emails per tenant, welcome emails for new tenant admins — SES in production, MailHog in dev
152	- No external auth service — Auth.js v5 sessions stored in PostgreSQL (white-label, zero external dependency)
153	
154	## Deployment Config
155	Environments: dev / staging / prod
156	Hosting:      Single VPS or managed services
157	Dev mode:     MODE A — WSL2 native (pre-locked)
158	Docker Hub:   enabled — hub_repo: bonitobonita24/inventorize
159	
160	## Mobile Needs
161	None — web only. The web app is mobile-first responsive (optimised for smartphones and tablets in warehouse environments) but there is no native mobile app.
162	
163	## Non-functional Requirements
164	Performance:    < 500ms API response for all CRUD operations. Stock transactions < 1s including all log writes. Tenant context resolution (slug → tenantId) < 10ms (cached).
165	Uptime:         99.5% SLA for production (SaaS — customer-facing)
166	Data retention: All traceability records kept minimum 7 years. Users disabled, never deleted. Tenants suspended, never deleted.
167	Compliance:     None specific. Standard data protection practices. Cross-tenant data isolation enforced at database level via RLS.
168	
169	## Tenancy Model
170	multi — subdirectory routing
171	URL pattern: app.com/[tenant-slug]/dashboard, app.com/[tenant-slug]/products, etc.
172	Shared global data: none — all business data is tenant-scoped
173	Roles: global (same role set across all tenants: admin, warehouse_staff, purchasing_staff)
174	DB isolation: shared schema + tenant_id column on all tenant-scoped tables + PostgreSQL RLS
175	DB isolation exception: none (inventory data does not require separate schema)
176	
177	## User-Facing URLs
178	/login                              public — Auth.js v5 authentication
179	/platform/tenants                   super_admin — tenant management
180	/platform/metrics                   super_admin — cross-tenant metrics
181	/platform/audit-logs                super_admin — platform audit logs
182	/[tenant-slug]/dashboard            tenant — main dashboard
183	/[tenant-slug]/products             tenant — product list
184	/[tenant-slug]/products/[id]        tenant — product detail
185	/[tenant-slug]/products/[id]/history tenant — product movement history
186	/[tenant-slug]/suppliers            tenant — supplier list
187	/[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders      tenant — PO list
188	/[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders/[id] tenant — PO detail
189	/[tenant-slug]/stock-in             tenant — stock receiving
190	/[tenant-slug]/stock-out            tenant — stock releasing
191	/[tenant-slug]/adjustments          tenant — stock adjustments (admin only)
192	/[tenant-slug]/reports              tenant — reports and exports
193	/[tenant-slug]/audit-logs           tenant — audit log viewer (admin only)
194	/[tenant-slug]/users                tenant — user management (admin only)
195	
196	## Access Control
197	Public routes:    /login (Auth.js v5 sign-in page)
198	Protected routes: all other routes require authenticated session via Auth.js v5
199	Super-admin only: /platform/* routes
200	Tenant admin only: /[tenant-slug]/users, /[tenant-slug]/audit-logs, /[tenant-slug]/adjustments
201	Tenant admin + purchasing: /[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders, /[tenant-slug]/suppliers
202	All tenant roles: /[tenant-slug]/dashboard, /[tenant-slug]/products, /[tenant-slug]/stock-in, /[tenant-slug]/stock-out, /[tenant-slug]/reports
203	
204	### Tenant isolation rules
205	- Authenticated user's tenantId is extracted from Auth.js v5 session/JWT on every request (L1)
206	- Every tenant-scoped DB query is automatically filtered by tenantId via Prisma middleware (L2)
207	- PostgreSQL RLS provides database-level enforcement as defense-in-depth
208	- A user assigned to tenant A can never access routes or data for tenant B
209	- super_admin has no tenantId — accesses only /platform/* routes, never tenant business data directly
210	
211	## Data Sensitivity
212	PII stored:       yes — user name, email, hashed password (in PostgreSQL via Auth.js v5), requestedByName
213	Financial data:   yes — supplierCost, markupPercent, sellingPrice, purchase order values, inventory valuation (restricted by role, scoped by tenant)
214	Health data:      no
215	Audit required:   tenant creation/suspension/reactivation, user creation/role changes/disabling, login events, product create/update/pricing/threshold changes, supplier create/update, PO create/update/status change, stock in, stock out, stock adjustment, serial number creation/status changes, file upload/removal, report export, notification send/failure, every quantity-affecting stock movement, super_admin impersonation events
216	GDPR/compliance:  no specific regulatory requirement — standard data protection. Cross-tenant data isolation enforced at DB level. 7-year retention. No hard deletes.
217	
218	## Security Requirements
219	Rate limiting:    public: 60/min per IP | authenticated: 300/min per user | upload: 10/min per user
220	CORS origins:     dev: localhost:* | staging: https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app | prod: https://inventorize.powerbyte.app
221	Security layers:  L1 app-layer tenant scoping (tenantId from Auth.js session/JWT) — ACTIVE
222	                  L2 Prisma auto-inject middleware (tenantId on every query) — ACTIVE
223	                  L3 RBAC tRPC middleware (role-based access on every endpoint) — ACTIVE
224	                  L4 PgBouncer per-tenant connection pool limits — ACTIVE
225	                  L5 AuditLog (immutable record on every mutation) — ACTIVE
226	                  L6 Prisma query guardrails (automatic data scoping) — ACTIVE
227	
228	### Inventory-specific security
229	- No negative stock — enforced at database transaction level
230	- Audit logs immutable — no update or delete operations permitted
231	- Stock movement logs immutable — no update or delete operations permitted
232	- Tenant admin cannot view other tenants' audit logs
233	- Warehouse staff cannot view supplier cost or markup pricing data
234	- All barcode/QR scanned values must pass server-side validation
235	- super_admin actions are audit-logged separately at platform level
236	
237	### Browser camera scanning
238	- Camera permission requested before use
239	- Client-side JavaScript barcode/QR library (html5-qrcode or zxing-js)
240	- Supported: Android Chrome, iOS Safari, laptop webcam
241	- Manual text input fallback always available
242	- Live scanner preview with clear success/error feedback
243	- Large tap targets for warehouse mobile use
244	
245	## Domain / Base URL Expectations
246	Dev:     http://localhost:[port assigned by Phase 3 — do not specify a number here]
247	Stage:   https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app
248	Prod:    https://inventorize.powerbyte.app
249	
250	## Infrastructure Notes
251	Default: all services run in Docker Compose — mono-server for dev/staging/prod.
252	Docker Hub publishing: enabled — hub_repo: bonitobonita24/inventorize
253	pgAdmin: included on all environments — credentials auto-generated by Phase 3
254	CREDENTIALS.md: generated by Phase 3 — master credentials list for all envs, strictly gitignored
255	Security: HTTP headers + rate limiter + DOMPurify sanitizer scaffolded by Phase 4 — always-on defaults
256	Spec stress-test: Phase 2.7 runs automatically before Phase 3 — catches PRODUCT.md gaps early
257	AWS path when ready: RDS, S3, ElastiCache, SES — update .env.{env} only, zero code changes.
258	
259	### Compose services (dev)
260	- Next.js app (web server + tRPC API)
261	- PostgreSQL (primary database with RLS policies — also stores Auth.js v5 sessions)
262	- Valkey (cache + BullMQ job queue)
263	- MinIO (S3-compatible file storage for uploads)
264	- pgAdmin (database management UI)
265	- PgBouncer (connection pooling with per-tenant limits)
266	
267	### Production path
268	- Managed PostgreSQL with RLS (e.g. RDS, Supabase, or DigitalOcean Managed DB)
269	- S3-compatible storage (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, or DigitalOcean Spaces)
270	- Managed Redis/Valkey (e.g. ElastiCache, Upstash)
271	- PgBouncer (connection pooling)
272	- SES or equivalent for email notifications
273	
274	## Tech Stack Preferences
275	Frontend framework:        Next.js
276	API style:                 tRPC
277	ORM / DB layer:            Prisma
278	Auth provider:             Auth.js v5 (sessions in PostgreSQL — white-label, zero external service)
279	Auth strategy:             authjs
280	Primary database:          PostgreSQL
281	Cache / queue:             Valkey + BullMQ
282	File storage:              MinIO (dev) / S3 (prod)
283	UI component library:      shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS
284	
285	## Design Identity
286	Brand feel:         professional/enterprise
287	Target aesthetic:   Clean, dense information layout optimised for fast warehouse operations. Minimal decorative elements. High contrast for readability in bright warehouse environments. Large tap targets and clear feedback for scanning workflows. Tenant branding kept minimal — platform-consistent UI.
288	Industry category:  SaaS / inventory management
289	Dark mode required: optional toggle
290	Key constraint:     Mobile-first responsive layout — warehouse staff primarily use smartphones and tablets. Scanner UI must have large tap targets and clear success/error feedback.
291	
292	## Out of Scope
293	- Multi-warehouse support per tenant (single location per tenant, no warehouse-to-warehouse transfers)
294	- Approval workflows (approvedByUserId on StockAdjustment is optional audit trail only — not blocking)
295	- Native mobile app (web app is mobile-first responsive, not a native iOS/Android app)
296	- Realtime push updates or WebSocket connections
297	- Advanced demand forecasting or predictive analytics
298	- Supplier analytics or supplier performance scoring
299	- Complex enterprise ERP features (accounting, general ledger, HR, payroll)
300	- Offline-first functionality (requires internet connection)
301	- Public API for external integrations
302	- Multi-language / i18n support
303	- Billing or invoicing (no subscription management, payment processing, or usage metering in this version)
304	- Customer self-service tenant registration (super_admin onboards tenants manually)
305	- Custom branding per tenant (single platform-wide look and feel)
306	- Tenant data export/migration tooling
307	- External IAM platform (no Logto, Auth0, or Clerk — Auth.js v5 handles all auth natively)
308
```
</details></tool-use>

📋 **PHASE 8 — NEXT BUILD BATCH PROPOSAL**

---

**Built so far (from IMPLEMENTATION_MAP.md):**
- ✅ Full monorepo scaffold (root config, 6 packages, 1 web app)
- ✅ 17 Prisma models with migration + seed data
- ✅ 10 tRPC routers with real business logic (CRUD, pagination, atomic transactions, RBAC)
- ✅ Auth.js v5 with JWT, login page, middleware
- ✅ All page routes with data-fetching UI (products, suppliers, POs, stock in/out, adjustments, reports, audit logs, users, platform admin)
- ✅ Tenant layout with sidebar navigation
- ✅ packages/jobs (BullMQ queues + worker factories)
- ✅ packages/storage (S3/MinIO operations + validation)
- ✅ Docker Compose (6 services), CI/CD, deploy scripts
- ✅ Security headers, rate limiting, DOMPurify sanitizer

**Not yet built (declared in PRODUCT.md but missing):**
- ⬜ **Barcode/QR scanning** — browser camera scanning via html5-qrcode for stock in/out/product lookup
- ⬜ **CSV export** — export reports as CSV files (all report types)
- ⬜ **Dashboard charts** — Stock In vs Stock Out bar chart (recharts or similar)
- ⬜ **File upload UI** — PO attachment upload, delivery receipt upload (storage package exists but no UI wiring)
- ⬜ **Printable stock out slip** — generate/print slip from stock out records
- ⬜ **Email notifications** — daily low stock email + welcome email for new tenant admins (SMTP/nodemailer integration)
- ⬜ **Super admin impersonation** — read-only impersonation of tenant admin for support
- ⬜ **Dark mode toggle** — optional theme toggle (PRODUCT.md Design Identity)
- ⬜ **Serial number UI flows** — scanning/selection in stock in/out forms (backend logic exists, UI needs wiring)

**Proposed next batch (highest value / most unblocking):**

1. **Barcode/QR scanning + serial number UI** — Core warehouse workflow. Stock in/out is the daily driver. Without scanning, users must manually type every product code and serial number. This unblocks real-world testing.

2. **CSV export for reports** — Every report page needs this. High user-facing value, relatively low complexity.

3. **Dashboard charts + KPI cards** — The dashboard is the landing page after login. Currently shows data but no visual charts. Adds immediate perceived value.

7	export default function DashboardPage() {

9	
10	  if (isLoading) {
11	    return <div className="p-4">Loading dashboard...</div>;
12	  }
13	
14	  const cards = [
15	    { label: 'Total Products', value: summary?.totalProducts ?? 0 },
16	    { label: 'Active Products', value: summary?.activeProducts ?? 0 },
17	    { label: 'Low Stock Items', value: summary?.lowStockProducts ?? 0 },
18	    { label: 'Inventory Value', value: `$${(summary?.inventoryValue ?? 0).toLocaleString()}` },
19	  ];
20	
21	  return (
22	    <div className="space-y-6">
23	      <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold">Dashboard</h1>
24	      <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 lg:grid-cols-4">
25	        {cards.map((card) => (
26	          <div key={card.label} className="rounded-lg border border-border p-4">
27	            <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{card.label}</p>
28	            <p className="text-3xl font-bold">{card.value}</p>
29	          </div>
30	        ))}
31	      </div>
32	    </div>
33	  );

 `./apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/report.router.ts`

```text
1	// Report router — tenant-scoped reporting queries
2	
3	import { z } from 'zod';
4	import { createTRPCRouter, tenantProcedure } from '../trpc';
5	import { prisma } from '@inventorize/db';
6	import { withTenantContext } from '@inventorize/db';
7	
8	export const reportRouter = createTRPCRouter({
9	  lowStock: tenantProcedure
10	    .input(
11	      z.object({
12	        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
13	        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
14	      }).strict(),
15	    )
16	    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
17	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion
18	      const tenantId = ctx.tenantId!;
19	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion
20	      const userId = ctx.userId!;
21	      return withTenantContext(
22	        { tenantId, userId },
23	        async () => {
24	          const [items] = await Promise.all([
25	            prisma.$queryRaw`
26	              SELECT id, "product_code" AS "productCode", name, "current_quantity" AS "currentQuantity",
27	                     "low_stock_threshold" AS "lowStockThreshold", unit, category
28	              FROM "Product"
29	              WHERE "tenant_id" = ${tenantId}
30	                AND "is_active" = true
31	                AND "current_quantity" <= "low_stock_threshold"
32	              ORDER BY ("current_quantity"::float / NULLIF("low_stock_threshold", 0)) ASC
33	              LIMIT ${input.limit} OFFSET ${(input.page - 1) * input.limit}
34	            ` as Promise<unknown[]>,
35	            prisma.product.count({
36	              where: {
37	                tenantId,
38	                isActive: true,
39	                currentQuantity: { lte: prisma.product.fields.lowStockThreshold as unknown as number },
40	              },
41	            }),
42	          ]);
43	
44	          // Fallback: use Prisma client for low stock count
45	          const lowStockCount = await prisma.product.count({
46	            where: {
47	              tenantId,
48	              isActive: true,
49	            },
50	          });
51	
52	          return { items, total: items.length, page: input.page, limit: input.limit, totalPages: Math.ceil(lowStockCount / input.limit) };
53	        },
54	      );
55	    }),
56	
57	  stockMovements: tenantProcedure
58	    .input(
59	      z.object({
60	        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
61	        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
62	        productId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
63	        movementType: z.enum(['stock_in', 'stock_out', 'adjustment']).optional(),
64	        startDate: z.string().datetime().optional(),
65	        endDate: z.string().datetime().optional(),
66	      }).strict(),
67	    )
68	    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
69	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion
70	      const tenantId = ctx.tenantId!;
71	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion
72	      const userId = ctx.userId!;
73	      return withTenantContext(
74	        { tenantId, userId },
75	        async () => {
76	          const where = {
77	            tenantId,
78	            ...(input.productId !== undefined ? { productId: input.productId } : {}),
79	            ...(input.movementType !== undefined ? { movementType: input.movementType } : {}),
80	            ...(input.startDate !== undefined || input.endDate !== undefined
81	              ? {
82	                  performedAt: {
83	                    ...(input.startDate !== undefined ? { gte: new Date(input.startDate) } : {}),
84	                    ...(input.endDate !== undefined ? { lte: new Date(input.endDate) } : {}),
85	                  },
86	                }
87	              : {}),
88	          };
89	          const [items, total] = await Promise.all([
90	            prisma.stockMovementLog.findMany({
91	              where,
92	              skip: (input.page - 1) * input.limit,
93	              take: input.limit,
94	              orderBy: { performedAt: 'desc' },
95	              include: {
96	                product: { select: { name: true, productCode: true } },
97	                performedByUser: { select: { name: true } },
98	              },
99	            }),
100	            prisma.stockMovementLog.count({ where }),
101	          ]);
102	          return { items, total, page: input.page, limit: input.limit, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / input.limit) };
103	        },
104	      );
105	    }),
106	
107	  inventorySummary: tenantProcedure
108	    .query(async ({ ctx }) => {
109	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion
110	      const tenantId = ctx.tenantId!;
111	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion
112	      const userId = ctx.userId!;
113	      return withTenantContext(
114	        { tenantId, userId },
115	        async () => {
116	          const [totalProducts, activeProducts, lowStockProducts, totalValue] = await Promise.all([
117	            prisma.product.count({ where: { tenantId } }),
118	            prisma.product.count({ where: { tenantId, isActive: true } }),
119	            // Count products where current quantity is at or below threshold
120	            prisma.product.count({
121	              where: {
122	                tenantId,
123	                isActive: true,
124	              },
125	            }),
126	            // Sum of currentQuantity * sellingPrice for all active products
127	            prisma.product.findMany({
128	              where: { tenantId, isActive: true },
129	              select: { currentQuantity: true, sellingPrice: true },
130	            }),
131	          ]);
132	
133	          const inventoryValue = totalValue.reduce(
134	            (sum, p) => sum + p.currentQuantity * p.sellingPrice.toNumber(),
135	            0,
136	          );
137	
138	          return {
139	            totalProducts,
140	            activeProducts,
141	            lowStockProducts,
142	            inventoryValue,
143	          };
144	        },
145	      );
146	    }),
147	});
148
```
</details></tool-use>

```tsx
'use client';

import { useEffect, useRef, useState, useCallback } from 'react';
import { Html5Qrcode } from 'html5-qrcode';

interface BarcodeScannerProps {
  onScan: (value: string) => void;
  onError?: (error: string) => void;
  placeholder?: string;
}

export function BarcodeScanner({ onScan, onError, placeholder = 'Type or scan barcode...' }: BarcodeScannerProps) {
  const [manualInput, setManualInput] = useState('');
  const [scanning, setScanning] = useState(false);
  const [scannerError, setScannerError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const scannerRef = useRef<Html5Qrcode | null>(null);
  const containerRef = useRef<string>('barcode-reader-' + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9));

  const stopScanner = useCallback(async () => {
    if (scannerRef.current !== null) {
      try {
        const state = scannerRef.current.getState();
        if (state === 2) { // SCANNING
          await scannerRef.current.stop();
        }
      } catch {
        // Scanner may already be stopped
      }
      scannerRef.current = null;
    }
    setScanning(false);
  }, []);

  const startScanner = useCallback(async () => {
    setScannerError(null);
    try {
      const scanner = new Html5Qrcode(containerRef.current);
      scannerRef.current = scanner;
      setScanning(true);

      await scanner.start(
        { facingMode: 'environment' },
        { fps: 10, qrbox: { width: 250, height: 150 } },
        (decodedText) => {
          onScan(decodedText);
          void stopScanner();
        },
        () => {
          // Ignore scan failures (frame-by-frame)
        },
      );
    } catch (err) {
      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Camera access denied';
      setScannerError(message);
      onError?.(message);
      setScanning(false);
    }
  }, [onScan, onError, stopScanner]);

  useEffect(() => {
    return () => {
      void stopScanner();
    };
  }, [stopScanner]);

  const handleManualSubmit = (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const trimmed = manualInput.trim();
    if (trimmed.length > 0) {
      onScan(trimmed);
      setManualInput('');
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="space-y-3">
      <form onSubmit={handleManualSubmit} className="flex gap-2">
        <input
          type="text"
          value={manualInput}
          onChange={(e) => { setManualInput(e.target.value); }}
          placeholder={placeholder}
          className="flex-1 rounded-md border border-border bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm"
        />
        <button
          type="submit"
          className="rounded-md bg-primary px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-primary-foreground"
        >
          Go
        </button>
        <button
          type="button"
          onClick={() => { scanning ? void stopScanner() : void startScanner(); }}
          className="rounded-md border border-border px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium min-w-[100px]"
        >
          {scanning ? '■ Stop' : '📷 Scan'}
        </button>
      </form>

      {scannerError !== null && (
        <p className="text-sm text-red-600">{scannerError}</p>
      )}

      <div
        id={containerRef.current}
        className={scanning ? 'rounded-lg overflow-hidden border border-border' : 'hidden'}
      />
    </div>
  );
}

```
